title: identity: secret_bytes() not compiler-enforced against logging; pattern propagates to callers
description: Identity::secret_bytes() and hpke_secret_bytes() carry SECURITY doc comments but return plain [u8;32]. Any future caller can accidentally log, serialize, or display these values — the compiler does not stop them. New contributors can copy the pattern without noticing the annotation. Consider wrapping in a newtype that implements neither Debug nor Display. This is distinct from nie-kef6.103 (zeroize): this covers the display/logging propagation risk.
